question: A machine that produces stampings for automobile engines is malfunctioning and producing $10 \%$ defectives. The defective and nondefective stampings proceed from the machine in a random manner. If the next five stampings are tested, find the probability that three of them are defective.
answer:
Let $x$ equal the number of defectives in $n=5$ trials. Then $x$ is a binomial random variable with $p$, the probability that a single stamping will be defective, equal to . 1 , and $q=1-p=1-.1=.9$. The probability distribution for $x$ is given by the expression

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(x) & =\binom{n}{x} p^x q^{n-x}=\binom{5}{x}(.1)^x(.9)^{5-x} \\
& =\frac{5!}{x!(5-x)!}(.1)^x(.9)^{5-x} \quad(x=0,1,2,3,4,5)
\end{aligned}
$$

To find the probability of observing $x=3$ defectives in a sample of $n=5$, substitute $x=3$ into the formula for $p(x)$ to obtain

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(3) & =\frac{5!}{3!(5-3)!}(.1)^3(.9)^{5-3}=\frac{5!}{3!2!}(.1)^3(.9)^2 \\
& =\frac{5 \cdot 4 \cdot 3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1}{(3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1)(2 \cdot 1)}(.1)^3(.9)^2=10(.1)^3(.9)^2 \\
& =.0081
\end{aligned}
$$

meta_info.caption: Applying the Binomial Distribution-Manufacture of Automobiles
